hq: 11726 San Vicente Blvd, Suite 600, Los Angeles, CA 90049

system_description.overview: MPL Risk AI develops AI-powered compliance automation tools designed to streamline vendor risk management, insurance compliance workflows, and other operational processes. The platform reduces manual review burden through workflow automation and configurable rules engines, serving enterprise teams in regulated and compliance-sensitive industries.

products.MPLRisk.ai platform: Core web platform serving as the central interface for compliance advisory and automation-readiness services
products.Vendor Due Diligence Automation Module: Planned - automates vendor onboarding through structured data collection, document ingest, and risk scoring
products.Third-Party Risk Monitoring Module: Planned - continuously evaluates vendors for legal, financial, cybersecurity, and reputational risks
products.Policy & Controls Enforcement Engine: Planned - allows customers to define and operationalize compliance rules (ESG, privacy, security)
products.Insurance Compliance Review Module: Planned - automates COI validation and exception handling
products.Vendor Lifecycle Management Module: Planned - manages end-to-end vendor interactions from onboarding through offboarding

infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices or servers; all data processed on cloud services
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (production servers in AWS maintain failover across all availability zones)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Hosted in AWS VPC environment. Network topology includes segmented VPCs and access control lists (ACLs). System components configured to appropriately segment customer accounts.
network_architecture.waf: AWS WAF (shown in network diagram: IAM -> WAF -> Web Application)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS employed via AWS to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with VPN
network_architecture.tls: TLS (Transport Layer Security) using certificates from established third-party certificate authority
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; all systems restricted to internet via this system
network_architecture.topology: User requests -> WAF -> Web Application -> Database; IAM controls access; Logging & Monitoring; External Integrations; all within AWS VPC environment

application_architecture.pattern: Monolith (single web application platform at early stage)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments (changes tested before deployment to Stage or Production)

data_storage.backup_frequency: Backup policy exists; frequency not specified

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS (passwords controlled through AWS Password Policy; Active Directory mentioned for infrastructure)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform; MFA required to access source code repository; MFA required for employee email access
authentication_access_control.password_policy: AWS password policy enforced; includes periodic forced changes, password expiry, complexity requirements; accounts disabled after limited failed logon attempts; guest/anonymous logins not allowed

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest
encryption.key_management: AWS KMS (cloud provider key management service)

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Source code management tool (unnamed; MFA-protected, branch rules configured)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to require additional approval for every merge request moving to production
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for baseline infrastructure as code (IaC) implementation and rollback capability
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management set up by CTO; monthly patch management reviews; workstations receive automatic updates and scanned daily for patch compliance; OS patches applied as critical/security patches become available

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Monitoring tool (unnamed) - continuously monitors system capacity and performance, generates alerts at predefined thresholds
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Centralized logging (tool not named); logs cover administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions, security configurations, permissions and roles
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured for high and critical risk events; incidents managed via incident management process

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure / data center services
TLS certificates for encrypted web traffic

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and malware protection software installed on all production servers (continuous auto-scan; definitions updated weekly for servers, daily for workstations) and Mac workstations/laptops
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; scan frequency adjustable; self-assessment scans prior to each production release or annually
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access to data center facilities (AWS responsibility as subservice org)
A1.2 - Environmental protections/data backup/recovery infrastructure (MPL Risk has no office; managed by building management/AWS)
Processing Integrity and Privacy trust service categories - determined not relevant to system
notable_controls: CC6.1.1 - MFA enforced for all accounts with administrative access to production
CC8.1.2 - Branch protection rules require additional approval on all merge requests to production
CC8.1.3 - Source code access restricted to MFA-authenticated authorized users
CC4.1.1 - Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
CC7.1.2 - Monthly automated vulnerability scanning on infrastructure and applications
CC6.2.2 - Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including privileged users
CC6.1.4 - Access revoked within 1 business day of termination
CC7.4.1 - BCDR policy documented, reviewed annually, and tested annually per defined testing schedule

infrastructure.rationale: AWS with multi-AZ failover and VPC is solid. No multi-region, no CDN mentioned, no specific region disclosed. Early-stage company with all infrastructure on AWS (single provider). IaC used but no specific tooling named.

application_architecture.rationale: Appears to be an early-stage monolithic web platform. No programming languages, frameworks, or compute services named. Separate environments exist and branch protection is configured, but very limited technical disclosure.

data_layer.rationale: Database exists on AWS but type not named. No caching, no data warehouse, no file storage specifics disclosed. Backup policy exists but frequency not stated. Data classification and retention policies present.

security_posture.rationale: Strong controls: WAF, IDS/IPS, monthly vuln scanning, annual external pen test, MFA on admin access, antivirus on all endpoints, quarterly access reviews, access revocation within 1 business day. Limited by lack of SIEM naming and no cybersecurity insurance disclosure.

devops_maturity.rationale: Separate environments, branch protection, IaC, automatic patch management, emergency change process. CI/CD tool not named, source control tool not named, no container orchestration disclosed. Moderate maturity but low disclosure.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented and tested annually with multiple scenarios. Multi-AZ failover in AWS. No RTO/RPO disclosed. No multi-region DR. Backup frequency not specified.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Heavy single-vendor concentration on AWS. Only AWS and a TLS certificate authority identified as third parties. Very limited vendor ecosystem disclosed - appropriate for early-stage company but represents concentration risk.

overall.rationale: Early-stage compliance-automation company with good governance foundations and solid security practices for its stage. However, most of the product modules are not yet in production (planned roadmap). The SOC 2 scope focuses primarily on the consulting/advisory platform and foundational controls rather than a mature SaaS product. Limited technical disclosure throughout.

red_flags: Most product modules are explicitly NOT in production - the SOC 2 covers a consulting/advisory platform, not the full planned SaaS product suite
No programming languages, frameworks, or compute services named - extremely limited technical disclosure
CI/CD tool and source control platform not named
yellow_flags: Single cloud provider (AWS) with no documented secondary provider
Backup frequency not specified in the report
Processing Integrity and Privacy TSC criteria excluded as 'not relevant' - unusual for a compliance automation platform handling customer data
green_flags: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
MFA enforced on production admin access and source code repository
Access revocation within 1 business day of termination
BCDR policy tested annually across multiple loss scenarios
Branch protection with mandatory code review approval for production
Comprehensive endpoint protection including AV on all production servers and workstations
key_observations: This is effectively a pre-product SOC 2 - the company secured certification for its consulting workflows and foundational controls before the actual software product is in production. This is strategically smart for enterprise sales but buyers should note the product roadmap items are not yet audited.

The network diagram shows a relatively simple architecture: IAM -> WAF -> Web Application -> Database, with logging/monitoring and external integrations as supporting components. No microservices complexity.
The CEO (Charles Hernandez) is also designated Head of Information Security, with two co-founders (Viet Dinh, Jonathan Choi) beneath. Very small team - key person risk for security oversight.
Content filtering via proxy server is noted for all systems - suggests a more traditional enterprise endpoint management approach rather than cloud-native zero trust.
The carve-out method for AWS means physical security, data center environmental controls, and availability zone infrastructure controls are explicitly out of scope.
